et: Descending air in high pressure = dry, stable, clear.
Winter rainfall from westerly cold fronts.

q: Contour lines connect points of equal:
et: Contour lines = equal altitude above sea level.
Warm Agulhas flows south along the east coast.

q: A cuesta's steep side is the:
et: Scarp slope is the steep eroded face; dip slope is gentle.
Desertification: fertile land becomes desert through overgrazing, deforestation, drought.
GIS: captures, stores, analyses geographic data.
Benguela: cold upwelling on west coast. Rich fishing, frequent fog.

q: SA's average annual rainfall is approximately:
et: SA average ~464mm — well below global average of 860mm.

q: The Coriolis force causes winds to deflect:
et: Coriolis: deflects left in Southern Hemisphere (opposite to Northern Hemisphere).

opts: Steady rain ahead of the front
et: Warm front: warm air rises over cold — steady prolonged rainfall.
Urbanisation: population shift from rural to urban areas.
SA Gini ~0.63 — one of the world's highest. Legacy of apartheid inequality.

q: A population pyramid with wide base indicates:
et: Wide base = many young people = high birth rate = developing country.
HDI: Human Development Index — composite of income, education, life expectancy.

q: Day Zero in Cape Town (2018) nearly happened because:
et: Cape Town's dams nearly ran dry at 13.5% capacity — averted by extreme restrictions.
The Great Escarpment runs along eastern SA — separating highveld plateau from coastal plains.

q: Which gas most contributes to the greenhouse effect?
et: CO₂ from burning fossil fuels is the primary greenhouse gas driver.
Push factors drive people AWAY from an area: poverty, unemployment, conflict.

q: The Lesotho Highlands Water Project transfers water to:
et: LHWP transfers water from Lesotho highlands to Vaal Dam — supplying Gauteng.

opts: A flat-topped hill with steep sides
et: Mesa: isolated flat-topped landform with resistant cap rock protecting softer rock below.

q: Global warming is primarily caused by:
et: Burning coal, oil, and gas releases CO₂ — the primary driver of anthropogenic warming.
Tectonic plates move — collisions build mountains, separations form rift valleys.
SA has 11 official languages — the most of any country in Africa.
